perturbation.original_text: For any Product or Solution sold to any perspective clients introduced by Bravatek registered with COMPANY via email to COMPANY's CEO and delivered through Bravatek or a COMPANY-designated distribution affiliate(s) or sales channel(s), Bravatek will receive a lead-finder fee, to be mutually discussed and finally decided by COMPANY at the range of minimum of 10% to maximum of 20% of project revenue, with an exact fee to be depending upon the overall project sales margin and cost of development and delivery of each project, payable NET 30 days after each client payment on delivered products received at COMPANY's bank account.
perturbation.changed_text: For any Product or Solution sold to any perspective clients introduced by Bravatek registered with COMPANY via email to COMPANY's CEO and delivered through Bravatek or a COMPANY-designated distribution affiliate(s) or sales channel(s), Bravatek will receive a lead-finder fee, to be decided by COMPANY.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific percentage range for the lead-finder fee, the modified text contradicts the idea of a minimum or maximum fee, creating uncertainty in the amount Bravatek will receive. The text still states that Bravatek *will* receive a fee, implying a concrete number, but omits the previously defined range. This creates an in-text contradiction between the promise of a fee and the lack of a defined amount, making enforcement difficult. The last part of the sentence was removed, as well, payable NET 30 days after each client payment on delivered products received at COMPANY's bank account
perturbation.contradicted_text: When custom Products are designed, developed and to be delivered to Bravatek-identified perspective clients, the parties shall agree to a proposed sales price for use during the project in writing prior to the commencement of each project. 2 For any Product or Solution sold to any perspective clients introduced by Bravatek registered with COMPANY via email to COMPANY's CEO and delivered through Bravatek or a COMPANY-designated distribution affiliate(s) or sales channel(s), Bravatek will receive a lead-finder fee, to be decided by COMPANY.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the previously agreed-upon range (10%-20%) for the lead-finder fee, stating it will now only be 'decided by COMPANY.' This directly contradicts the original text's specification of a minimum and maximum percentage, creating uncertainty about the actual fee Bravatek will receive. The contradicted text contains the changed text so it is an obvious contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: COMPANY and Bravatek agree to maintain in strict confidence and not to disclose or disseminate, or to use for any purposes other than performance of the projects, the Confidential Information disclosed.
perturbation.changed_text: COMPANY and Bravatek agree to maintain in confidence and not to disclose or disseminate, or to use for any purposes other than performance of the projects, the Confidential Information disclosed.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of 'strict' before 'confidence' introduces ambiguity. While still requiring confidentiality, the level of strictness is now undefined. This weakens the obligation and could lead to disputes about what constitutes a breach of confidentiality. This will contradict Section 6, since in this section no level of strictness is defined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For a period of five (5) years after termination of this Agreement, the parties shall treat as confidential all information and take every reasonable precaution and use all reasonable efforts to prevent the unauthorized disclosure of the same. The parties agree to take all steps reasonably necessary and appropriate to ensure that their employees, agents, and/or assistants treat all information as confidential and to ensure that such employees, agents, and/or assistants are familiar with and abide by the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text required 'strict confidence,' which implies a high level of protection for Confidential Information. The changed text removes 'strict,' weakening the obligation. The contradicted text states that the parties shall "take every reasonable precaution and use all reasonable efforts to prevent the unauthorized disclosure of the same." Without the 'strict' qualifier, the standard of care required to maintain confidentiality becomes ambiguous, potentially conflicting with the explicit requirement to take "every reasonable precaution and use all reasonable efforts," thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement is twelve (12) months from the date hereof, and will be automatically renewed for one (1) additional twelve month period unless either party shall notify the other in writing of its intention not to renew. Such notice must be given ninety (90) days prior to expiration of the original term.
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement is twelve (12) months from the date hereof, and may be renewed.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the 'automatic renewal' clause, the modified text creates uncertainty regarding the agreement's duration after the initial term. The original text specifies an automatic renewal unless a 90-day notice is given. The modified text only states that the agreement 'may be renewed' without specifying how or under what conditions this renewal would occur. This contradicts an expectation of continued collaboration established elsewhere in the agreement, creating an in-text contradiction because there is no renewal conditions now.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term of this Agreement is twelve (12) months from the date hereof, and may be renewed. This Agreement may also be terminated by either party upon ninety (90) days written notice. 3
perturbation.justification: The original text specified an automatic renewal process, while the changed text removes this automaticity and vaguely states that the agreement 'may be renewed' without outlining any specific conditions or procedures. The contradicted text also refers to a renewal, but the changed text removes the previous conditions which provided clarity to the renewal process. By removing the automatic renewal conditions, it introduces ambiguity about the renewal process which creates contradiction, leading to uncertainty about obligations and rights of each party with an expectation of continued collaboration, creating an in-text contradiction.
